user_message:
You are running as part of compound-engineering:ce-doc-review. Review the document `docs/plans/2026-06-20-002-docs-comprehensive-project-manual-plan.md`.

Document type: plan
Origin: none
Decision primer: <prior-decisions>Round 1 — no prior decisions.</prior-decisions>

Return ONLY valid JSON with keys: reviewer, findings, residual_risks, deferred_questions. Findings must use severity P0/P1/P2/P3, finding_type error/omission, autofix_class safe_auto/gated_auto/manual, confidence 50/75/100 only, and evidence as an array of direct quotes from the document. Suppress style-only or speculative findings. Focus only on plan-level security: sensitive data, secrets, tokens, privacy, sync/export trust boundaries, redaction, and threat-model gaps.
assistant_summary: {"reviewer":"ce-security-lens-reviewer","findings":[{"title":"LLM data boundary under-specified","severity":"P2","section":"Proposed Manual Outline / 7. Configuration and Environment","why_it_matters":"Readers configuring optional LLM backends need more than placeholder token examples: they need to know what prompt, session, decision, or assessment data can leave the local machine and what redaction applies before that transfer. The plan mentions tokens and redaction separately, but does not require a single trust-boundary statement for external LLM providers.","finding_type":"omission","autofix_class":"gated_auto","suggested_fix":"Add an acceptance check to the configuration or agent-integration work item requiring the manual to document optional LLM backend trust boundaries: credential source, whether data leaves the machine, minimum outbound data, redaction before requests, local/disabled alternatives, and failure behavior.","confidence":50,"evidence":["- API tokens and optional LLM backends at a safe, placeholder level.","- Redaction and prompt handling boundaries."]}],"residual_risks":["Most likely exploit if execution is careless: a real token or private path is copied into a config example; the plan mitigates this with placeholder-only examples and manual secret/private-data searches.","Highest-impact exploit if execution is careless: no-filter sync/export is documented without a clear warning and a user publishes unredacted memory artifacts; the plan already calls for filtered/no-filter sync paths plus privacy/local-only considerations, so verify this in the final manual.","Most subtle exploit if execution is careless: generated .agent-loop artifacts influence prose and leak private prompt context; the plan limits them to evidence prompts and requires private-data checks, so this remains an execution review point."],"deferred_questions":["Should the final manual explicitly name environment variables such as OPENAI_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN as credential sources, rather than implying tokens belong in repo-local config?","Should the sync chapter include a danger callout for --no-filter or equivalent no-filter paths?"]}
